steps.title: Match the claim
steps.content: The claim says Newland compares his intellect favorably with that of other men in New York society. The supporting quotation must therefore show him viewing himself as intellectually superior, not merely describing the opera setting.

steps.title: Reject off-target quotes
steps.content: A discusses social inferiority and moral doctrine without a direct intellectual comparison. B only describes Newland looking across the opera house. D concerns how people arrive at the opera. The remaining quotation says he had read and thought more than the others. The answer is C.
